content.problemStatement:
Salmon loves to be a tidy person. One day, when he looked at the mess that he made after playing with his rubber ducks, he felt awful. Now he wants to clean up his mess, by placing his ducks into boxes.

Each rubber duck has a color. There are a total of N+1N+1N+1 colors, numbered from 000 to NNN. Salmon wants to place his N∗KN*KN∗K ducks into NNN boxes, each of which can fit KKK ducks. Each duck should be placed inside a box.

Salmon is very particular when it comes to how many colors he can place in each box. Since Salmon doesn't like lots of colors mixing together he only wants to have a maximum of 222 distinct colors per box.

Please help Salmon achieve this goal!

It can be shown that there will always be at least one valid solution under given constraints.

If there are multiple correct solutions, you may output any one of them.

Input:
The first line contains an integer TTT, denoting the number of testcases. TTT testcases will follow, each containing two lines.
The first line of each testcase contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line of each testcase contains N+1N+1N+1  space-separated integers. The i+1i+1i+1-th integer denotes the number of ducks with color-iii where 0≤i≤N0 \leq i \leq N0≤i≤N
Output:
Output NNN lines for each testcase.
The iii-th line of a testcase should contain 444 space-separated integers c1,m1,c2,m2c1, m1, c2, m2c1,m1,c2,m2 respectively which denotes that that are m1m1m1 ducks of color-c1c1c1 and m2m2m2 ducks of color-c2c2c2 inside the iii-th box where 0≤m1,m2≤K0 \leq m1,m2 \leq K0≤m1,m2≤K and 0≤c1,c2≤N0 \leq c1,c2 \leq N0≤c1,c2≤N.
Note that even if you have only one color to put inside the iii-th box, you should still output 444 space-separated integers and keep either m1m1m1 or m2m2m2 as 000. And 0≤c1,c2≤N0 \leq c1,c2 \leq N0≤c1,c2≤N.
The output should be valid and should satisfy Salmon's goal.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: the first line contains two integers N and K. The second line contains N+1 integers a0 a1 … aN where ai is the number of ducks of color i. It is guaranteed that Σ ai = N·K.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output N lines. The i‑th line contains four space‑separated integers c1 m1 c2 m2 describing the i‑th box: m1 ducks of color c1 and m2 ducks of color c2 (0 ≤ m1,m2 ≤ K, 0 ≤ c1,c2 ≤ N). If a box uses only one colour set the other pair to 0 (e.g., c2=0,m2=0). Each box must contain exactly K ducks and at most two distinct colours.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are 3 ducks of colour 0, 4 of colour 1 and 1 of colour 2. Box 1 receives 3 ducks of colour 0 and 1 duck of colour 1. Box 2 receives 3 ducks of colour 1 and 1 duck of colour 2. Each box contains exactly 4 ducks and at most two colours.
Each colour has exactly 2 ducks and each box can hold 2 ducks, so every box is filled with a single colour.

hidden.label: edge case with many zero‑count colours

solution.approach: We fill the boxes one by one. Keep an index cur pointing to the smallest colour that still has remaining ducks. For each box we first take as many ducks as possible from colour cur (up to K). If the box is not yet full we move cur to the next colour with a positive remainder and take the remaining needed ducks from that colour. Because we use at most two colours per box, the constraint is satisfied. After a colour is exhausted we advance cur for the next box. The total number of operations is O(N+K) per test case, i.e. linear in the number of colours.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N + K) per test case, overall O(T·(N+K)).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) to store the remaining counts.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it))
        rem = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N+1)]
        cur = 0
        for _ in range(N):
            while cur <= N and rem[cur] == 0:
                cur += 1
            take1 = min(rem[cur], K)
            rem[cur] -= take1
            c1 = cur
            need = K - take1
            c2 = 0; take2 = 0
            if need:
                while cur <= N and rem[cur] == 0:
                    cur += 1
                take2 = min(rem[cur], need)
                rem[cur] -= take2
                c2 = cur
            out_lines.append(f"{c1} {take1} {c2} {take2}")
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
